measure_name: Energy efficiency obligation scheme
short_description: Energy efficiency obligation scheme. Obligated parties – energy suppliers – implement measures contributing to decarbonisation of buildings (advanced metering, monitoring and optimisation of consumption, installation of photovoltaic systems, etc.) and purchase energy savings from third parties such as ESCO companies. Activities valued through administrative increase of up to 30% in savings achieved for energy poverty measures.
quantified_objective: Not specifically quantified in the measure table.
planned_budget_and_sources: Not specifically stated; sources: EU ETS revenues, supplier's fees.
authorities_responsible: MINGO
expected_impacts: Contributing to building renovation, decarbonisation of buildings, and addressing energy poverty.
implementation_status: In implementation - referenced across multiple sections without dedicated table

measure_name: Promoting decarbonisation and applying the energy efficiency first principle in buildings
short_description: The measure builds on the Charter of Cooperation for the Decarbonisation of Buildings by 2050, strengthens inter-departmental cooperation between the public and private sectors through workshops and dialogues, focusing on the renovation of buildings and the construction of nZEB and ZEB performance, the reduction of emissions, the increase of RES and the uptake of smart technologies.
authorities_responsible: Ministry of Physical Planning Construction and State Assets (MPGI)
expected_impacts: Network of experts and partners, continued cooperation on building renovation strategy, transition to nZEB standard, energy efficiency first info-campaign.
implementation_status: In implementation. Updates required: extension of topics in line with EU guidelines – focus on the energy efficiency first principle, boosting energy and comprehensive renovation of buildings, new EPBD requirements (minimum energy performance standards, renovation trajectories for residential buildings, minimum energy performance, urban mobility, solar systems, use of RES, ZEB, etc.).

measure_name: Energy renovation programme for multi-apartment buildings
short_description: Scheme adopted by Decision of the Croatian Government for the period up to 2030 (NN No 143/21). It encompasses integral, deep and comprehensive renovation. Three models: reconstruction of intact, reconstruction of earthquake-damaged, support to citizens at risk of energy poverty. An incentive to renovate buildings according to nZEB and ZEB performance is also planned. Encourage energy and comprehensive renovation which, in addition to energy efficiency measures, fosters an increase in the earthquake-proofness of the building, safety in case of fire, healthy indoor climate conditions, application of green infrastructure, urban mobility, accessibility for persons with disabilities and reduced mobility, including the use of RES, connection to efficient centralised systems, etc. New fossil fuel systems are not encouraged.
quantified_objective: Restore 6.27 million m2 in total by 2030, averaging around 700,000 m2 per year. Savings in 2030: 1.87 PJ of energy; reducing CO2 emissions: 124.88 kt. Cumulative until 2030: 8.45 PJ and 565.87 ktCO2.
type_of_policy_or_measure: Financial measure; grants and financial instruments (loans)
planned_budget_and_sources: NRRP (39.8 million EUR), ERDF (89 million EUR), an additional 120 million EUR (RePowerEU), EPEEF resources. A total of over 54.5 million EUR for the reconstruction of earthquake-damaged.
authorities_responsible: MPGI – development and monitoring of implementation; monitoring: MINGO - Monitoring of realised impacts and reporting
expected_impacts: Reducing energy consumption in multi-apartment buildings, increasing RES, reducing CO2 emissions. Increasing resilience to earthquakes and fires.
implementation_status: In implementation. Updates required: developing models to help homeowners at risk of energy poverty; differentiation of support for worst performing buildings and for other buildings; energy communities and energy sharing.

measure_name: Energy renovation programme for family houses
short_description: Continuation of the Family Houses Renovation Programme (2014-2020). Continuous implementation through annual public calls is foreseen. Models: reconstruction of intact, earthquake-damaged, family homes of vulnerable citizens at risk of energy poverty. The possibility of implementing individual measures in a given order. New fossil fuel systems shall not be incentivised. A strong promotional component.
quantified_objective: Restore 11.5 million m2 by 2030 (average 13,500 houses per year, 1.35 million m2). Savings: 0.447 PJ per year; by 2030 a total of 20.26 PJ and 1,356.16 ktCO2.
planned_budget_and_sources: Total investment cost 3.44 bn EUR (2021-2030). Sources: revenue from the EU ETS, supplier's fees, EPEEF funds, additional 120 million EUR. 25 million EUR (EPEEF 2023) for energy poor; additional funding will come from the Social Climate Fund (measure MS-15).
authorities_responsible: MPCA – programming and monitoring of implementation; EPEEF – annual public calls; MINGO – Monitoring of realised impacts and reporting
expected_impacts: Reduction of thermal needs, increase of RES, reduction of CO2 emissions. Strengthening the resilience of family houses and energy security.
implementation_status: In implementation. Updates required: differentiation of support for worst performing and other buildings, fostering renovation to nZEB performance, e-mobility, other forms of sustainable mobility, use of RES, fostering green infrastructure.

measure_name: Energy renovation programme for public buildings
short_description: Continuation of the 2016-2020 Programme Adopted by decision of the Croatian Government for the period up to 2030 (NN No 41/22). Combination of market models (ESCO) and grants. Focus on central government buildings (e.g. hospitals, penitentiaries, old people's homes), educational/educational facilities (schools, kindergartens). The objective is to renovate to nZEB and ZEB properties, i.e. deep renovation. Encourage energy and comprehensive renovations that, in addition to energy efficiency measures, encourage an increase in the seismic resilience of the building, fire safety, healthy indoor climate conditions, the use of green infrastructure measures, urban mobility, accessibility for persons with disabilities and reduced mobility; including use of RES; connection to efficient centralised systems, etc. New fossil fuel systems are not incentivised.
quantified_objective: Renovation of 350,000 m2 public buildings per year. Savings: annually 0.169 PJ; by 2030 a total of 9.30 PJ and a reduction in CO2 emissions of 264.93 ktCO2.
type_of_policy_or_measure: Financial measure; ESCO model and grants
planned_budget_and_sources: NRRP, AC 2021-2027, EPEEF funds, ERDF. Investment cost 1.2 bn EUR (2021-2030).
authorities_responsible: MPGI; APN for ESCO models; EPEEF; MINGO – Monitoring of realised impacts and reporting
expected_impacts: Reducing energy consumption and CO2 emissions, increasing the use of RES. Developing energy services markets and mobilising private capital.
implementation_status: In implementation. Updates required: meeting the MEPS, renovation of worst-performing buildings, measures for buildings with good energy performance with a view to achieving the ZEB standard, renovation obligations for individual public bodies under the Energy Efficiency Directive; mandatory implementation of the ESCO model for large buildings or building complexes.

measure_name: Energy renovation programme for buildings with the status of a cultural good
short_description: The Programme for the energy renovation of buildings with the status of cultural property for the period up to 2030 (NN No 143/21) was adopted by decision of the Croatian Government. It covers individually protected cultural goods (buildings and assemblies) and buildings within protected cultural and historical units. It does not cover preventively protected or recorded cultural goods. The programme provides for a holistic (integrated) approach to the energy and comprehensive renovation of buildings, other improvement measures as well as an approach through individual renovation measures.
quantified_objective: Cumulative savings in the period up to 2030: 6.32 PJ; reduction of CO2 emissions in 2030: 245.48 ktCO2.
planned_budget_and_sources: EUR 2.5 billion (1.8 billion investment + 0.7 billion maintenance) in 2021-2030; sources: EU funds, monument rent, NRRP.
authorities_responsible: MPGI – competent authority for the Programme, MKM – implementing authority; monitoring: MINGO - Monitoring of realised impacts and reporting
expected_impacts: Reducing thermal needs and energy consumption, increasing the use of RES, reducing CO2 emissions.
implementation_status: In implementation. Need for update: in line with the new EPBD requirements and co-financing possibilities.

measure_name: Systematic energy management in the public sector
short_description: The measure is based on a public sector obligation for systematic energy management. Implementation includes collection and analysis of energy consumption data, planning of measures and reporting. NRRP funds were supported (pilot project to establish an ISGE for multi-dwelling buildings).
type_of_policy_or_measure: Information and organisational measure; grant from the NRRP for a pilot project
planned_budget_and_sources: EUR 1.59 million from the NRRP for the pilot project for the set-up and implementation of the SGE (C6.1. R6).
authorities_responsible: APN; other bodies in the EU funds system; MPGI
expected_impacts: Energy savings in the public sector, better planning and implementation of energy efficiency measures.
implementation_status: Implemented through a pilot project financed by the NRRP; continued implementation under the Energy Efficiency Act. Need for update: it is necessary to upgrade the EMIS with all the functionalities needed to fulfil the obligations of both public authorities and the APN under the new Energy Efficiency Act.

measure_name: Green Public Procurement
short_description: The measure promotes the implementation of green public procurement in the Republic of Croatia. It includes defining mandatory sustainability criteria in procurement procedures, educating public buyers and fostering markets for sustainable products and services.
authorities_responsible: MINGO; public procurement authorities
expected_impacts: Reducing resource and energy consumption through public procurement, boosting the market for green products and services.
implementation_status: In implementation. Need for update: green public procurement criteria should be expanded in line with the EED requirements, which are transposed through the new Energy Efficiency Act.

measure_name: Information on energy efficiency
short_description: The measure includes the implementation of information activities on energy efficiency, on the need for comprehensive renovation and decarbonisation of buildings, targeted at citizens and businesses. The aim is to raise awareness and encourage the implementation of energy efficiency and renewable energy measures, risk resilience (fire, earthquake, climate change, etc.).
authorities_responsible: The competent ministries in cooperation with the EPEEF
expected_impacts: Increased awareness of citizens and businesses, reduction of energy consumption through voluntary activities.
implementation_status: In implementation. Need for updates: OSS network, monitoring the effects of campaigns.

measure_name: Developing a framework to ensure adequate skills in the context of green jobs needed for building renovation
short_description: The measure aims at ensuring adequate knowledge and skills needed to implement the green transition, with a focus on building renovation and capacity building for energy efficient and sustainable projects – this is a reform measure C6.1. R2 from the NRRP.
quantified_objective: Not specifically quantified in the document.
planned_budget_and_sources: The envisaged use of EU funds, including the National Recovery and Resilience Plan (NRRP) and other funds.
authorities_responsible: Ministry of Science Education and Youth – implementing body for reform C6.1. R2 from the NRRP; ASOO – co-financing training of training centres for workers; CES – co-financing training/reskilling unemployed; The Faculty of Construction in Zagreb as part of the CROSKILLS project; MPGI – setting up and applying the green building principle in cooperation with the Croatian Green Building Council and Architecture Faculty (AF)
expected_impacts: Strengthening labour market capacity through the development of green skills necessary for the implementation of energy renovation measures in buildings.
implementation_status: In implementation. Updates required: implement the measures in accordance with the National Guidelines.

measure_name: Increasing the efficiency of the heat system
short_description: Provision is made for the replacement of the end-of-life distribution network in CTS with new pre-insulated pipes and a technological shift towards a fourth generation of district heating. In smaller systems planned reconstruction of boiler room (cogeneration, heat pumps, geothermal). Development of new heating and cooling systems for RES and CHP. It also includes the introduction of advanced metering of consumption.
planned_budget_and_sources: An estimate of resources has yet to be made; sources: CTS operators, EU funds.
authorities_responsible: Producers, distributors and suppliers of heat from CTS
expected_impacts: Reduction of losses in CTS, efficiency gains, integration of RES and flexibility of energy systems.
implementation_status: Implementation 2021-2030. Need for update: CTS operators need to adopt plans to transform the system into high-efficiency in line with the requirements of the Energy Efficiency Directive – the implementation of plans and the Heat Market Act need to be monitored.

measure_name: Increasing energy efficiency and RES use in enterprises
short_description: The measure refers not only to the manufacturing industry but also to undertakings in the commercial and service sectors, providing favourable credits for energy efficiency improvement measures, which may also include the renovation of buildings. Implemented by HBOR and funded by the 2021-2027 Competitiveness and Cohesion Programme.
quantified_objective: Not specifically quantified in the measure table.
planned_budget_and_sources: Funded by the 2021-2027 Competitiveness and Cohesion Programme through HBOR.
authorities_responsible: HBOR (Croatian Bank for Reconstruction and Development)
expected_impacts: Increasing energy efficiency and the use of RES in enterprises, including building renovation in commercial and service sectors.
implementation_status: In implementation - referenced without dedicated table

measure_name: Adoption and implementation of the Programme to combat energy poverty
short_description: Design of programmes to combat energy and mobility poverty. It includes mitigating the degree of vulnerability, setting up monitoring systems, and capacity building through local info-centres.
planned_budget_and_sources: Is not precisely assessed; funding from the commitment system, EU funds, the ETS of revenues and the Social Climate Fund.
authorities_responsible: MINGO; Ministry of the Sea, Transport and Infrastructure
expected_impacts: Setting up a system for monitoring, reducing energy consumption and CO2 emissions in energy- and mobility-poor households.
implementation_status: Not implemented. Updates required: the Programme should be developed and adopted.

measure_name: Implementation of the Programme for Combating Energy Poverty in Residential Buildings in Assisted Areas and Areas of Special State Care
short_description: The programme covers energy efficiency measures and the use of RES in residential buildings in assisted areas and areas of special state care. It covered 387 buildings (297,575 m2).
quantified_objective: Primary energy savings: 27 GWh/year; electricity generation from RES: 4,360 MWh/year; reduction of CO2 emissions: 691 t/year.
planned_budget_and_sources: EUR 88.32 million; Recovery and Resilience Facility, State budget (solidarity fee), Energy Efficiency Obligation Scheme Obliged Entities, EPEEF, EU funds.
authorities_responsible: MPGI
expected_impacts: Energy renovation of buildings, use of RES, reduction of CO2 emissions, improvement of living conditions in social housing.
implementation_status: In implementation 2022-2025 (planned extension). Updates required: in the first phase of implementation, the funds were not sufficient for all buildings foreseen by the Programme; resources should be provided to fully meet the objectives – to this end, resources should be provided for in the Social Climate Plan (MS-15); the programme should also be extended until 2030.

measure_name: Drafting and implementing the Social Climate Plan
short_description: The Social Climate Plan is a document submitted to the European Commission by 30 June 2025 to benefit from the Social Climate Fund. The Plan shall contain a set of national measures and investments to mitigate the negative impacts of the EU ETS2 system on vulnerable households, vulnerable micro-enterprises and vulnerable transport users, ensuring affordable heating, cooling and mobility and accelerating measures to reach the EU climate targets.
planned_budget_and_sources: EUR 1.68 bn; sources: Social Climate Fund, revenues from the sale of EU ETS2 allowances.
authorities_responsible: Enforcement authorities: EPEEF, Ministry of Environmental Protection and Green Transition with cooperation with the Ministry of Finance; the Ministry of Physical Planning Construction and State Assets; the Ministry of the Sea Transport and Infrastructure; and the Ministry of Labour Pension System Family and Social Policy
expected_impacts: Reducing energy poverty, increasing energy efficiency in buildings and transport, more accessible sustainable mobility, reducing greenhouse gas emissions.
implementation_status: Planned action (draft Roadmap developed); implementation in 2026-2030. The plan entails the implementation of actions ENU-3 for citizens at risk of energy poverty and UET-10 in areas of special state care.

measure_name: National Housing Policy Plan 2030 and Action Plan 2025-2027
short_description: The first umbrella strategy paper on housing in Croatia, focusing on affordable and sustainable housing. It includes financial, tax, land and environmental energy instruments to increase housing availability, stabilise markets and social fairness.
quantified_objective: By 2030: 8,000 new units through APN; 2,000 in assisted territories; 1,200 through land policy and cooperatives; 9,000 through affordable renting; 65,000 energy renovated households.
type_of_policy_or_measure: Strategic and legislative framework with financial and organisational instruments
planned_budget_and_sources: 2025-2027: 683.6 million EUR; 2025-2030: EUR 2.03 bn. Sources: State budget, EU funds, Urban Development Fund, Revolving Fund from 2028.
authorities_responsible: Ministry of Physical Planning Construction and State Assets; APN; LRSGUS
expected_impacts: Increasing housing availability and affordability, reducing energy poverty, stabilising markets, activating empty buildings, reducing emissions through energy renovation.
implementation_status: Adopted on 26 March 2025, the implementation of the actions of the 2025-2027 Action Plan starts.

measure_name: Use of RES for heating purposes
short_description: Providing financial incentives for the development of projects for the use of RES for thermal purposes. Activities include financial support from EPEEF, analysis of geothermal potential (AWD), support for the development of geothermal energy projects, incentives for heat pumps, biomass projects, etc.
planned_budget_and_sources: EUR 1 billion for the whole period. Sources: The EU ETS revenues, the EU funds, the EEA and the Norwegian Financial Mechanism.
authorities_responsible: MINGO, FZOEU, Ministry of Agriculture Forestry and Fisheries, AZU, MRRFEU, HERA
expected_impacts: Increasing the use of biomass, biogas, geothermal energy and heat pumps; reducing greenhouse gas emissions.
implementation_status: Planned implementation 2023-2026. Updates required: the measure should be implemented systematically.

measure_name: Use of RES in district and closed heat systems
short_description: Enable an increase in the share of RES in CTS using geothermal, solar, water and other sources. Critical technologies: heat pumps with thermal reservoirs. It includes the fine-tuning of laws and regulations, the definition of conditions for seasonal tanks, the mapping of heat zones and the plan for the expansion of the water network, and the co-financing of plans and investments.
planned_budget_and_sources: EUR 750 million. Sources: EPEEF (EU ETS revenue), Modernisation Fund, EU funds.
authorities_responsible: EPEEF, MINGO, MPGI (responsible for spatial planning)
expected_impacts: Increasing the share of RES in heat, reducing CO2 emissions, increasing energy efficiency.
implementation_status: Planned implementation 2025-2030. Updates required: the measure should be implemented systematically.

measure_name: Energy sharing and energy communities
short_description: The measure provides for the establishment of a regulatory and financial framework for the establishment and operation of citizen energy communities and renewable energy communities, with a view to enabling the sharing and sharing of energy from renewable sources. It includes the improvement of network access rules, the settlement of energy exchanges and the exchange of metering and billing data between users and system operators. It includes support for technical documentation, investment investments and the operational functioning of energy communities, in particular in multi-apartment and public buildings.
quantified_objective: Increase the number of active energy communities and energy sharing schemes; increase the share of RES in final electricity consumption in buildings.
planned_budget_and_sources: Budgetary resources, EPEEF, HROTE, Cohesion Fund, Modernisation Fund, Recovery and Resilience Plan, EEA and Norwegian Financial Mechanisms, other sources.
authorities_responsible: MINGO (legislative framework), HERA (regulatory framework and barrier analysis), HEP-ODS (network access and billing rules), EPEEF (financial instruments), HROTE (operational aid)
expected_impacts: Enabling the sharing of electricity between users and within buildings; increasing the share of RES in final consumption; developing local models of self-consumption; strengthening energy independence and involving citizens in the energy transition.
implementation_status: Implementation in 2025-2030. Need for update: ensure implementation of the measure, in particular for multi-dwelling buildings.

measure_name: Alternative fuels infrastructure development programme for road transport
short_description: The objective of the measure is to encourage the purchase of new alternative fuel vehicles (electricity, hydrogen and other alternative fuels) and the development of the necessary infrastructure for their use. The measure contributes to reducing emissions in road transport, strengthening energy security and expanding the market for alternative fuels.
quantified_objective: Estimated savings in 2030: 0.74 PJ of energy; reduction of CO2 emissions: 361.7 ktCO2e. Cumulative energy savings in 2021-2030: 2.76 PJ; cumulative CO2 emissions reduction: 1,532.9 ktCO2e.
planned_budget_and_sources: Not specifically mentioned in the document.
authorities_responsible: MINGO, MMPI, EPEEF
expected_impacts: An increase in the number of electric recharging points for light-duty and heavy-duty vehicles in residential areas, the TEN-T network and urban nodes; increasing the number of hydrogen refuelling stations; increasing the share of alternative fuel vehicles; energy savings and reduction of CO2 and pollutant emissions.
implementation_status: Implementation in 2021-2030. Updates required: /

measure_name: Improving the sustainability of urban environments
short_description: The Programme for the Development of Green Infrastructure in Urban Areas 2021-2030 and the Programme for the Development of Circular Management of Space and Buildings 2021-2030 aim to integrate the energy, environmental and social aspects of urban development, including mobility, green infrastructure, waste and water management and circular management of space.
quantified_objective: Not numerically specified in the document.
planned_budget_and_sources: The envisaged use of EU funds and national funding sources is not specifically developed.
authorities_responsible: MPGI in cooperation with JLP(R)S
expected_impacts: Making urban environments more sustainable, developing green infrastructure, integrating the circular economy, reducing the impact of climate change and raising citizens' quality of life.
implementation_status: In implementation. Updates required: /
